docs.title: fcount-rs
docs.text: Ultrafast feature counting for RNA-seq data. A high-performance Rust alternative to featureCounts.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
fcount-rs is 2× faster than featureCounts with 8 threads on typical RNA-seq data:

Tool Threads Time (s) Speedup featureCounts 1 3.34 — featureCounts 8 1.45 — fcount-rs 1 2.26 1.5× fcount-rs 8 0.71 2.0×

Benchmark: chr22 subset (~1.4M paired-end reads). featureCounts v2.1.1.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Fast: 2× faster than featureCounts with parallel processing, custom GTF parser, and efficient data structures
  • Pure Rust: No C dependencies, uses the noodles library for BAM/SAM parsing
  • Compatible: Drop-in replacement for featureCounts with similar CLI interface
  • Flexible: Supports single-end and paired-end data, stranded protocols, and multi-mapping reads

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
cargo install fcount-rs

# Count reads
fcount-rs -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text: fcount-rs --help

docs.title: Single-End RNA-seq
docs.text: fcount-rs -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Paired-End RNA-seq
docs.text: fcount-rs -p -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Stranded Library (e.g., dUTP method)
docs.text: fcount-rs -p -s 2 -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Multi-Threaded Processing
docs.text: fcount-rs -T 8 -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Multiple BAM Files
docs.text: fcount-rs -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample1.bam sample2.bam sample3.bam

docs.title: Count Multi-Mapping Reads
docs.text:
fcount-rs -M -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

With fractional counting:

fcount-rs -M --fraction -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Feature-Level Counting
docs.text:
Count at exon level instead of gene level:

fcount-rs -f -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: Quality Filtering
docs.text:
Require minimum MAPQ of 10 and ignore duplicates:

fcount-rs -Q 10 --ignore-dup -a annotation.gtf -o counts.txt sample.bam

docs.title: From crates.io
docs.text: cargo install fcount-rs

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/rnabioco/fcount-rs
cd fcount-rs
cargo build --release

The binary will be at target/release/fcount-rs.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Rust 1.70+ (uses edition 2021)
  • Linux or macOS (uses jemalloc on non-MSVC platforms)

docs.title: Basic Command
docs.text: fcount-rs -a <annotation.gtf> -o <output.txt> <bam_files>...

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Option Description -a, --annotation <PATH> GTF/GFF annotation file -o, --output <PATH> Output count matrix file <BAM_FILES> One or more BAM/SAM input files

docs.title: Counting
docs.text: Option Description Default -t, --type <TYPE> Feature type to count exon -g, --gene-id <ATTR> GTF attribute for gene ID gene_id -f, --feature-level Count at feature level instead of gene level off

docs.title: Paired-End
docs.text: Option Description Default -p, --paired Count fragments instead of reads off -B, --both-aligned Require both mates aligned off -C, --count-chimeric Count chimeric fragments off --min-frag-len <INT> Minimum fragment length 50 --max-frag-len <INT> Maximum fragment length 600

docs.title: Strandedness
docs.text: Option Description Default -s, --strand <MODE> Strand mode: 0=unstranded, 1=stranded, 2=reversely stranded 0

docs.title: Multi-Mapping
docs.text: Option Description Default -M, --multi-mapping Count multi-mapping reads off --fraction Use fractional counting off --primary Count primary alignments only off

docs.title: Overlap
docs.text: Option Description Default -O, --multi-overlap Allow reads assigned to multiple features off --min-overlap <INT> Minimum overlapping bases 1 --frac-overlap <FLOAT> Minimum fraction of read overlapping feature 0.0 --frac-overlap-feature <FLOAT> Minimum fraction of feature overlapped 0.0 --largest-overlap Assign to feature with largest overlap only off

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text: Option Description Default -Q, --min-mapq <INT> Minimum mapping quality 0 --ignore-dup Ignore duplicate reads off

docs.title: Performance
docs.text: Option Description Default -T, --threads <INT> Number of threads 1

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Option Description -R, --details <PATH> Output detailed read assignments -q, --quiet Suppress progress output
